finding: The blind multi-angle round did not deliver: three blind subagents (13 angles) were spawned with diff-only context and all three went idle without returning findings, despite four explicit requests. The audit below was therefore performed by the author, which is WEAKER than the blind round the process calls for — recorded rather than papered over.

The TEST-6 assertion claimed titleUpdated always precedes the terminal frame, but the driver forwards the chunk stream and the extension channel through ONE tokio::select! which picks arbitrarily among ready branches; the test failed under --test-threads=4 while the title was correctly persisted. Flaky assertion removed; production is unaffected (long-lived per-conversation SSE + a Conversation/Update sync event).

seedConversation POSTed /messages without model_id, which SendMessageRequest marks required — every seeded message would 422, no user message would exist, and all four e2e specs would fail with a confusing 'row shows Untitled' assertion instead of the real cause. Now seeds a provider+model, passes model_id, and fails loudly on a non-ok response.

describe_advertised_tools rendered THIRD-PARTY tool names (an MCP server names its own tools) straight into a log line, so a name containing newlines could forge additional log lines. Now routed through the existing sanitize_prompt_field, which collapses control characters.

The same diagnostic was unbounded: a deployment advertising hundreds of tools rendered all of them into one log line. Capped at 40 entries with the omitted count reported, so truncation is explicit rather than silent.

LATERAL join reviewed: LEFT JOIN LATERAL (... LIMIT 1) ON TRUE contributes exactly one row per conversation so it cannot inflate COUNT(bm.message_id); fm.text added to GROUP BY because Postgres infers functional dependency only for columns of c; ORDER BY inside the lateral has a deterministic tiebreak (created_at, m.id, sequence_order). Verified by sqlx compile-time checking and by TEST-16 asserting message_count is unchanged.

first_message_preview newly returns MESSAGE CONTENT on the list endpoint. Scoping reviewed: the chat query is gated by WHERE c.user_id = $1 and the project query by c.user_id = $2 (defense-in-depth on top of the handler's ownership check), so a caller only ever receives previews of conversations it already owns. No new endpoint, no new permission.

Hook placement reviewed: called inside the existing `if let Some(registry)` scope in both break arms, before the terminal chunk, and in the CompleteWithContent arm after the append_content that persists the answer (so assistant_produced_output sees it). The error/failsafe breaks are deliberately not covered — a failed turn must not be titled, and the failsafe path already ran finalize on its prior iteration.

call_after_llm_skipped returns unit and swallows per-extension errors by design; safe at both call sites because the user's answer is already persisted and streamed by then. Contrast documented inline against call_after_llm_call, which can afford `?` because finalize's caller degrades to Complete. Covered by TEST-3.

Adding Option<String> to ConversationResponse is additive on the wire; the field lands on the projection type, not the Conversation row model, so no other consumer changes. OpenAPI regenerated for BOTH the server and desktop binaries (4 generated files) — required or the types_ts_parity golden test fails.

No reactive store proxy read was introduced inside a .map()/loop/conditional; conversationDisplayLabel is a pure function over data already in the rendered row, so the Rules-of-Hooks hazard does not apply.

The aria-label now carries the derived label, which is a genuine improvement: a screen-reader user previously heard N identical 'Untitled Conversation' rows. The label is capped at 120 chars server-side so it does not become an unreadably long accessible name.
